entry.title: Re: Undeploy or Delete OPC UA Server (shop floor system)
entry.content:
Hi @CC33,

Once you click the delete button in "Configure Production Connection," it will prompt you to create a new deployment group. Please create a new deployment group and deploy it from "Deploy Shop Floor Elements.
Once you deploy it shop floor Elements will be deleted. Once it is deleted you can delete able to delete both deployed groups.

Please let me know if you are still facing any issues. If this works for you, I would appreciate it if you could accept my answer.

Reagrds,
Kartik Singhal

entry.title: Asset Revaluation & Acquisition in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition via SAP Build Process Automation
entry.content:
This blog is intended to complement my previous blogs on automation and augment Finance Asset Accounting scenario with SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Public Edition as described with available scope items for Asset Revaluation (J62) and SAP Help documentation

A Professional Services company wanted to streamline its operations, particularly in managing the intricate logistics of end-to-end fixed asset acquisition and posting asset revaluation processes. 

Company discovers asset revaluation with seamless automation. By effortlessly collecting data from Asset Accountants through various document formats, including PDFs, we streamline the entire process.

In comprehensive approach, company identify assets acquired both in the current fiscal year and previous fiscal years. Each revaluation includes meticulous details such as Ledger information, Fixed Asset Revaluation Acquisition Amounts, Current Year Amounts, and the increments by which the original acquisition costs (APC) are adjusted.  In addition, specific details around corresponding cumulative value for ordinary revaluation are included.

SAP Build Process Automation's low-code solutions, designed to empower users to create and manage processes and workflows effortlessly. With the no-code process builder, enhancing enterprise applications and business sites is as simple as drag-and-drop.

 

Automation for asset accounting is driven by SAP Build Process Automation Actions (API), and rapidly developed UI-based automations for both SAP and third-party applications using the built-in automation recorder.

Decision management capabilities transform corporate policies and business rules into repeatable processes. The intuitive business rules editor lets users create decision tables, similar to spreadsheets, or text-based rules.

To streamline access and enhance operations, SAP Build Process Automation offer a unified launchpad and task center, providing a seamless user experience. This allows business users to easily access and manage notifications and tasks from various solutions, whether via web or mobile.

In a custom scenario evaluated by the Professional Services company, SAP Build Process Automation demonstrates how it can simplify process and task automation for business users without any coding required. The platform's built-in AI capabilities facilitate intelligent document processing, effortlessly extracting data from both structured and unstructured documents, and seamlessly transferring it to your enterprise systems.

In specific customer business use case, SAP Build Process Automation enhance accuracy by using document templates instead of relying solely on pre-trained models. This is achieved by uploading the business document to the service and creating a document template artifact, which can extract data from various file types (e.g., PDFs) as required for the specific business case.

Company evaluates the SAP Build Process Automation to elevate the efficiency and precision of their Asset management business operations, providing a robust solution for intelligent document processing and seamless integration with enterprise systems.

Enterprise Automation with SAP serves as the foundational layer for integrating Generative AI into your business processes. By embedding AI models into orchestrated workflows and task automations, you can significantly enhance the level of automation and efficiency in your operations. You can learn more with roadmap items

In future enhancements, customer plan to explore the benefits of AI-powered artifact creation and recommendations, areas not covered in the current scope.

  • Generative AI will streamline the creation of process and rules artifacts, leading to more efficient process analysis, automation, and integration capabilities.
  • AI-powered tools will generate integration flows that support seamless scaling and address deviations through advanced anomaly detection.

You can stay ahead of the curve by leveraging the latest AI advancements to enhance the efficiency and effectiveness of your business operations.

Furthermore, Event-Driven Architectures with SAP Build Process Automation empower business users to fully automate processes in response to business events. (see Business Accelerator Hub for more details).  You can utilize existing business event objects or design custom ones to monitor and subscribe to specific occurrences within your system of records, proactively triggering automations.

SAP Build Process Automation seamlessly integrates Document Information Extraction capabilities, effortlessly extracting relevant data from both structured and unstructured documents, such as scans.

Key data points, including asset acquisition date, useful life, business transaction type, and debit/credit indicators, are captured to facilitate year-end closing and the display of transactions in the asset history sheet.  It adeptly recognizes whether a revaluation is an increase or a reduction, and accurately extracts Revaluation Amounts for both APC (Acquisition Costs) and Ordinary Depreciation.

Leveraging AI-based document processing, SAP Build Process Automation enhances document extraction with features like automatic template detection and prebuilt activities for efficient data extraction. The system also can support data enrichment, like linking asset and company information from templates to match codes in SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Public Edition, and can automatically align text.

This AI-driven approach to document information extraction and recommendations underscores customer’s commitment to improving their end-user experience and maintaining a competitive edge in the market.

Consider a typical business scenario where automating asset revaluation and verifying asset posted transactions in specific ledger journal entries is essential for an Asset Accountant.

Using SAP Build Process Automation's tasks, it becomes reality to leverage API “Fixed Asset – Post Asset Revaluation” (API_FIXEDASSETREVALUATION_G4BA) to streamline the revaluation process for multiple assets in a single request. This not only enhances data accuracy but also reduces costs compared to manual revaluation entries.

API “Fixed Asset – Post Asset Revaluation”

The automation action task accesses the API, incorporating nearly all fields from the Fiori app “Fixed Asset – Post Asset Revaluation” (F7775), allowing Asset Accountants to re-valuate assets for selected ledgers and depreciation areas. Revaluations can be applied to acquisition and production costs (APCs) and ordinary depreciation amounts.

To re-valuate an asset, specify the revaluation amount by which the original APCs are to be increased and the corresponding cumulative value for ordinary depreciation. The system then posts the increased APC and ordinary depreciation for the selected ledgers and depreciation areas.

Acquisition and Production Costs encompass any asset acquired or purchased externally, including the invoice price and associated expenses such as customs, octroi, and freight. By factoring in these additional costs, you can determine the total acquisition cost essential for the capitalization of the asset.

Learn more about this efficient automation process by exploring the SAP Help API Documentation.

Next, the Asset Accountant wants to validate the revaluation postings with a list of journal entries and understand the depreciation applied to each asset using an explanation of the depreciation key configuration.

SAP Build Process Automation enables the automation of complex scenarios with ease using the recorder. By automatically capturing the "Manage Fixed Asset" application, it allows for the simultaneous design of automations, leveraging enhanced recording features. These features include recording applications combining screens on SAP GUI for Windows and UI automation technologies, enabling a seamless session that includes both Windows and Web (including UI5) interfaces.

Using a UI-based automation bot scheduled at the desired business frequency, the bot navigates to the “Manage Fixed Assets (New)” Fiori App for specific assets with revaluated postings. The bot executes UI-based navigation with a scheduled job to generate and export the list of journal entries for revaluation postings. This list includes details such as Asset Transaction Type, Ledger, Line Item, Posting Date, Valuation Date, and references, which are then emailed to business users including the Asset Accountant.

Additionally, the bot emails the Asset Accountant and defined business users an AI-assisted explanation of the Depreciation Key Configuration. This provides a better understanding of how depreciation keys operate and the rationale behind the system’s complex depreciation calculations for the specific asset.

An AI-powered, user-friendly explanation of depreciation keys facilitates quicker asset onboarding, enhances business user productivity in tracking asset value throughout its lifecycle and revaluation postings, and streamlines periodic activities. This efficiency reduces the time required to understand depreciation and asset values, driving overall operational effectiveness.

AI-assisted Explanation of Depreciation Key Configuration  (J62 - Beta) 

This AI powered feature is available only  BETA Customers & Partners and you can  find more information, on how  Get Involved Early and register @ Influence portal and experience the latest AI features in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition and influence upcoming AI developments.

In this business use case, the company provides assets, such as laptops and ergonomic office furniture, to consultants and project resources. Asset Accountants face the ongoing challenge of entering newly acquired assets and their inventory numbers and IMEIs into the system before distribution and activation for depreciation.

To address this, the company evaluates and customizes the SAP Build Process Automation Bot template to suit the specific needs of Asset Accountants for mass asset acquisition and creation.

The Asset Accountant uploads a pre-filled template to trigger an approval workflow, sending a notification to the Asset Manager for review. Upon the Asset Manager's approval, the automation posts the acquisition of assets in the SAP S/4HANA Cloud system. The Asset Accountant then receives a detailed report.

Mass Fixed Asset Acquisition SAP Build Process Automation Template

Additionally, SAP Build Process Automation template content streamlines the posting of asset acquisitions by fetching existing unposted fixed assets from the SAP S/4HANA Cloud system into a template file, which users update with the necessary asset information. The updated file is then used to extract asset data and utilize the Fixed Asset Acquisition APIs to post the assets. This automation not only saves time and reduces manual errors but also improves overall efficiency. For more details, refer to the documentation on the  SAP Business Accelerator Hub.

As the company continues to acquire new assets, it must create and maintain main and sub-asset numbers on an ongoing basis. To support this, SAP Build Process Automation includes API-driven action tasks to facilitate the creation of main asset number (Learn more about Fixed Asset - Create Main Asset (Asynchronous) with API Documentation, create an sub asset Fixed Asset - Create Sub Asset (Asynchronous) (Learn more with API Documentation)

In specific scenarios, Asset Managers need to update existing assets to reflect changes in master records and valuation information.

SAP Build Process Automation’s API-based Action Task automates these updates, including modifications to the company code, main asset number, asset sub-number, origin information, account assignment, ledger details, valuation data, and time-based valuation information. This streamlined process ensures accuracy and efficiency. For more details, refer to the Fixed Asset - Change (Asynchronous). API documentation .

For more information about the extensibility of the Manage Fixed Assets app, see App Extensibility: Manage Fixed Assets (Documentation)

Visit the SAP Business Accelerator Hub to learn more about 3900+ prebuilt integration scenarios, 1300+ business events, over 250+ connectors to SAP and non-SAP applications, 1200+ automation content, and 35+ integrations all in one place, the SAP Business Accelerator Hub.

Don’t hesitate to leave a comment and check out our community page to ask your questions and engage with the experts. Follow the PSCC_Enablement tag to stay up to date with our latest blog posts.

Stay tuned for more updates in the next quarters!

Follow us via @sap and #S4HANA, or myself via@har1234 (Hardeep Tulsi)

Stay tuned for more updates in the next quarters!

entry.title: Re: Issue when publishing content from WPB/SEN
entry.content: WPB 9.5 SP1???? 😮😖 It's out of mainstream maintenance since 2019. If this is true (you still run SAP WPB 9.5.1), then consider upgrading it immediately to any of the supported versions of SAP Enable Now, that definitely doesn't suffer from this bug. Upgrade takes less than hour provided you have skilled resources. 😉

entry.title: Re: How change the formate date of EXCEL templete in SAP IBP
entry.content:
Hi,

you have to maintain PERIODDESC attribute in the time profile before importing it in IBP. Hereunder an example for a Technical Week in which year and month are included in the specific format.

TPLEVELPERIODIDPERIODSTARTPERIODENDPERIODDESC
1530512018-10-31 00:00:00.0002018-10-31 23:59:59.000TW44a 2018-10

Off course you have to apply your format.

BR.

entry.title: Va02 BDC recording to excel file upload program for mass number of sales order
entry.content:
Using BDC record for va02 to create a program for uploading Excel file to change mass sales order line item wise to set reason for rejection value. It changes first line item for every sales order and for others Its give a message user 'Sales document 19 is currently being processed by user.' .

Please guided me to solve it.Codes given here as your reference:

REPORT zsd_va02_reason_rejection.


TABLES:vbak,vbap.
TYPE-POOLS truxs.


SELECTION-SCREEN SKIP 1.
SELECTION-SCREEN BEGIN OF BLOCK a1 WITH FRAME TITLE TEXT-001.
  PARAMETERS: p_file TYPE localfile OBLIGATORY.
SELECTION-SCREEN END OF BLOCK a1.
SELECTION-SCREEN SKIP 2.
SELECTION-SCREEN BEGIN OF BLOCK B1 WITH FRAME TITLE TEXT-004.

*SELECTION-SCREEN ULINE.
*SELECTION-SCREEN COMMENT /1(79) TEXT-002.
SELECTION-SCREEN COMMENT /1(79) TEXT-003.
SELECTION-SCREEN END OF BLOCK B1.


TYPES: BEGIN OF ty_data,
         vbeln   LIKE vbak-vbeln,
         posnr   LIKE vbap-posnr,
         abgru   LIKE vbap-abgru,
         message TYPE string,
       END OF ty_data.


DATA: it_data TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF ty_data.
DATA: gt_data TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF ty_data.


*TYPES: BEGIN OF ty_display,
*         vbeln   LIKE vbak-vbeln,
*         posnr   LIKE vbap-posnr,
*         abgru   LIKE vbap-abgru,
*         message TYPE string,
*       END OF ty_display.
*
*DATA: gt_data TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF ty_display,
*      gw_wa   TYPE ty_display.


DATA : gt_fcat    TYPE slis_t_fieldcat_alv,
       gs_fcat    LIKE LINE OF gt_fcat,
       gs_layout  TYPE slis_layout_alv,
       gs_variant TYPE disvariant,
       gv_cnt     TYPE i.


DATA: it_bdcdata TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF bdcdata,
      wa_bdcdata TYPE bdcdata.

*DATA:it_message TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF bdcmsgcoll.
*data:wa_message like it_message.
DATA:it_message LIKE bdcmsgcoll  OCCURS 0 WITH HEADER LINE.


INITIALIZATION.

AT SELECTION-SCREEN ON VALUE-REQUEST FOR p_file.

  DATA: file_name TYPE ibipparms-path.

  CALL FUNCTION 'F4_FILENAME'
    IMPORTING
      file_name = p_file.


START-OF-SELECTION.

  DATA: i_tab_raw_data TYPE truxs_t_text_data.
*       i_filename type

  CALL FUNCTION 'TEXT_CONVERT_XLS_TO_SAP'
    EXPORTING
*     I_FIELD_SEPERATOR    = abap_true
      i_line_header        = abap_true
      i_tab_raw_data       = i_tab_raw_data
      i_filename           = p_file
*     I_STEP               = 1
    TABLES
      i_tab_converted_data = it_data
    EXCEPTIONS
      conversion_failed    = 1
      OTHERS               = 2.
  IF sy-subrc <> 0.
* Implement suitable error handling here
  ENDIF.



  LOOP AT it_data INTO DATA(wa_data).

CLEAR: it_message.

    PERFORM bdc_dynpro      USING 'SAPMV45A' '0102'.
    PERFORM bdc_field       USING 'BDC_CURSOR'
                                  'VBAK-VBELN'.
    PERFORM bdc_field       USING 'BDC_OKCODE'
                                  '=ENT2'.
    PERFORM bdc_field       USING 'VBAK-VBELN'
                                  wa_data-vbeln.
    PERFORM bdc_dynpro      USING 'SAPMV45A' '4001'.
    PERFORM bdc_field       USING 'BDC_OKCODE'
                                  '=POPO'.
    PERFORM bdc_field       USING 'BDC_CURSOR'
                                  'RV45A-MABNR(01)'.
    PERFORM bdc_dynpro      USING 'SAPMV45A' '0251'.
    PERFORM bdc_field       USING 'BDC_CURSOR'
                                  'RV45A-POSNR'.
    PERFORM bdc_field       USING 'BDC_OKCODE'
                                  '=POSI'.
    PERFORM bdc_field       USING 'RV45A-POSNR'
                                  wa_data-posnr.
    PERFORM bdc_dynpro      USING 'SAPMV45A' '4001'.
    PERFORM bdc_field       USING 'BDC_OKCODE'
                                  '=ITEM'.
    PERFORM bdc_field       USING 'BDC_CURSOR'
                                  'VBAP-POSNR(01)'.
    PERFORM bdc_field       USING 'RV45A-VBAP_SELKZ(01)'
                                  'X'.
    PERFORM bdc_dynpro      USING 'SAPMV45A' '4003'.
    PERFORM bdc_field       USING 'BDC_OKCODE'
                                  '=SICH'.
    PERFORM bdc_field       USING 'BDC_CURSOR'
                                  'VBAP-ABGRU'.
    PERFORM bdc_field       USING 'VBAP-ABGRU'
                                  wa_data-abgru.

    REFRESH it_message.

    CALL TRANSACTION 'VA02' USING it_bdcdata MODE 'N'
          UPDATE 'A'
          MESSAGES INTO it_message.

*    CLEAR: it_bdcdata,wa_bdcdata,wa_data-message.


    LOOP AT it_message.

      CALL FUNCTION 'MESSAGE_TEXT_BUILD'
        EXPORTING
          msgid               = it_message-msgid
          msgnr               = it_message-msgnr
          msgv1               = it_message-msgv1
          msgv2               = it_message-msgv2
          msgv3               = it_message-msgv3
          msgv4               = it_message-msgv4
        IMPORTING
          message_text_output = wa_data-message.

*      MODIFY gt_data FROM gw_wa TRANSPORTING message.
      APPEND wa_data TO gt_data.
      EXIT.

      CLEAR : it_message,wa_data.
    ENDLOOP.


    CLEAR: it_bdcdata,wa_bdcdata,wa_data,it_message.

  ENDLOOP.

END-OF-SELECTION.




  gv_cnt = gv_cnt + 1.
  gs_fcat-col_pos     = gv_cnt.
  gs_fcat-fieldname   = 'VBELN'.
  gs_fcat-seltext_m   = 'VBELN'.
  APPEND gs_fcat TO gt_fcat.
  CLEAR : gs_fcat.

  gv_cnt = gv_cnt + 1.
  gs_fcat-col_pos     = gv_cnt.
  gs_fcat-fieldname   = 'POSNR'.
  gs_fcat-seltext_m   = 'POSNR'.
  APPEND gs_fcat TO gt_fcat.
  CLEAR : gs_fcat.


  gv_cnt = gv_cnt + 1.
  gs_fcat-col_pos     = gv_cnt.
  gs_fcat-fieldname   = 'ABGRU'.
  gs_fcat-seltext_m   = 'ABGRU'.
  APPEND gs_fcat TO gt_fcat.
  CLEAR : gs_fcat.

  gv_cnt = gv_cnt + 1.
  gs_fcat-col_pos     = gv_cnt.
  gs_fcat-fieldname   = 'MESSAGE'.
  gs_fcat-seltext_m   = 'MESSAGE'.
  APPEND gs_fcat TO gt_fcat.
  CLEAR : gs_fcat.



  CALL FUNCTION 'REUSE_ALV_GRID_DISPLAY'
    EXPORTING
*     I_CALLBACK_PROGRAM                = sy-repid
*     I_CALLBACK_PF_STATUS_SET          = ' '
*     I_CALLBACK_USER_COMMAND           = 'USER_COMMAND'
      is_layout     = gs_layout
      it_fieldcat   = gt_fcat
      i_save        = 'A'
      is_variant    = gs_variant
    TABLES
      t_outtab      = gt_data
    EXCEPTIONS
      program_error = 1
      OTHERS        = 2.
  IF sy-subrc <> 0.
* Implement suitable error handling here
  ENDIF.


FORM bdc_dynpro USING program dynpro.
  CLEAR wa_bdcdata.
  wa_bdcdata-program  = program.
  wa_bdcdata-dynpro   = dynpro.
  wa_bdcdata-dynbegin = 'X'.
  APPEND wa_bdcdata TO it_bdcdata.
ENDFORM.


FORM bdc_field USING fnam fval.
*  IF FVAL <> NODATA.
  CLEAR wa_bdcdata.
  wa_bdcdata-fnam = fnam.
  wa_bdcdata-fval = fval.
  APPEND wa_bdcdata TO it_bdcdata.
*  ENDIF.
ENDFORM.

entry.title: SAP Build sessions for week 2 of Devtoberfest
entry.content:
👉𝗔𝗿𝗲 𝘆𝗼𝘂 into UI5 and GenAI (AI) and want to see a sneak peek how SAP Build Apps might support it?


👉𝗔𝗿𝗲 𝘆𝗼𝘂 a full-stack developer working with CAP or UI5 and want to know the proper way to prepare your app to be consumed across BTP tenants?


👉𝗔𝗿𝗲 𝘆𝗼𝘂 an experienced backend or frontend developer, or citizen developer, who wants to quickly build sophisticated mobile apps for both iOS and Android, using SAP Build Code and without needing to know Swift or Android/Kotlin tools?


👉𝗔𝗿𝗲 𝘆𝗼𝘂 an SAP developer or citizen developer who wants to learn how to put all the BTP services together – Integration Suite, SAP Build, AI – to create real-world sophisticated S/4HANA extensions?


Enjoy today's 𝗧𝗼𝗼𝗹𝗶𝗻𝗴 𝗧𝘂𝗲𝘀𝗱𝗮𝘆 (𝗦𝗔𝗣 𝗕𝘂𝗶𝗹𝗱) sessions, and continue your Devtoberfest experience!

entry.title: Re: Image pixels for Manage Recruiting Sites
entry.content:
Hi Sourabh, 

There are no general guidelines mentioned for the Pixel images. However I would like to recommend this KBA and it has a note https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/2264945

Note: In terms of the correct size, this is depending on general image sizing guidelines and how large you want to display it. This is not SF specific, as every customer's image sizing is different.

Regards,

Sadiya

entry.title: How to show Selected Branch name in General Ledger SAP B1 Standard Report PLD
entry.content: To open General Ledger directionI have selected all criteriaHere I have select Branch optionOpened Branch list and selected one branchThen Ok, then Ok, then Ok button click to open general ledger data listHere showing all data listTo open General ledger layout for click File then click Preview LayoutsSelected general ledger and then OK then ContinueChoose Layout of General Ledger (SA) then click OKHere opened General Ledger SAP B1 Standard Layout PLD, here red mark place, I want to show selected branch name or company name but there will condition please see the image.this is edited layout here I want to show branch name in red mark place.

entry.title: Re: 🟡 Techtoberfest 2024
entry.content: Basic but definitely gets the job done. Looking forward to the upcoming learning this week.

entry.title: Re: How to setup SAP CDC Solution in Azure Data Factory
entry.content: The ADF SAP CDC Connector has been on the market since 2022, so not quite new. SAP support note 3255746 targets tools such as ADF SAP CDC Connector which uses the ODP RFC API. The newest connector is the ADF OData CDC Connector that is in private preview.

entry.title: Re: Export and import ATC objects across on-premise and cloud ABAP systems

entry.title: Re: Quiz: incorrect number of selections
entry.content:
The error message "incorrect number of selections" means

  • Example the question says there are three (3) correct answers of the question, while the user only chooses one (1) or two (2) of them

The solution is

  • we select more answers, which matches the expected number of selections, the error message will be gone automatically and the submit button will be enabled again

Re: Deployment of ML Models in SAP AC
entry.content:
You can host your ML models on platforms like AWS, Azure, or Google Cloud, and expose them via APIs. SAC can consume these APIs to fetch predictions and integrate them into your reports.

Use SAP BW to prepare and transform your data as needed for your ML models. Once the data is ready, you can export it to your ML environment for training and scoring.

Thanks

Savio Dmello

entry.title: MTA and PNPM combined
entry.content:
When working with a monorepository for your BTP CloudFoundry application, using a package manager like pnpm can be highly beneficial. However, switching to a different package manager requires adapting your build and deployment configurations, specifically in the mta.yaml file for BTP CloudFoundry. With pnpm, each UI component and CAP service are managed as separate packages, unlike a typical CAP application where everything is contained within a single application with various UI components in the App folder. With pnpm, the structure would look more like this:

  • Package folders: the main folder with each UI Component and CAP application (could be more then one).

This structure is a bit different compared to a typical CAP application and requires some changes in the configuration files.

In the mta.yaml file, you'll need to adjust the before-all build commands. Instead of using npm install and cds build, you'll need to replace these with equivalent pnpm commands. Specifically, use pnpm to execute npm install within the CAP package by applying a filter with the pnpm command. Similarly, update the build command to leverage pnpm.

https://github.com/marianfoo/ui5con24-dev-env/blob/aa4d2051b0b82728a889ebaf431ca2b211af1ac0/mta.yaml#L174

 

The pnpm build script will execute the build script in the cap package which is in the packages folder:

https://github.com/marianfoo/ui5con24-dev-env/blob/aa4d2051b0b82728a889ebaf431ca2b211af1ac0/packages/cap/package.json#L28

 

The mta.yaml configuration for the CAP module is normally configured to use “npm” as builder. It is not possible to change this to pnpm. This might cause problems when your CAP services has dependencies to other pnpm packages. Anyway, keep “npm” as builder:

https://github.com/marianfoo/ui5con24-dev-env/blob/aa4d2051b0b82728a889ebaf431ca2b211af1ac0/mta.yaml#L21

 

The npm build  script of the CAP project (in before-all of mta.yaml) copies the package.json including the devDependencies which cannot be resolved when it contains references to other packages. It cannot resolve the dependencies because the workspace references can only be resolved by pnpm. The default mta build uses npm for the service and cannot be changed to pnpm:

https://github.com/marianfoo/ui5con24-dev-env/blob/aa4d2051b0b82728a889ebaf431ca2b211af1ac0/packages/cap/package.json#L11

 

As a solution you have to install the npm package  “cds-plugin-ui5” to the CAP app, which will remove the workspace dependencies when building. Special thanks to @pmuessig for this plugin:

https://github.com/marianfoo/ui5con24-dev-env/blob/aa4d2051b0b82728a889ebaf431ca2b211af1ac0/packages/cap/package.json#L9

 

For the UI packages, the mta.yaml configuration needs to be adapted as well. In all html5 modules, the commands “npm install” and “npm run build:cf” have to be replaced with pnpm commands:

https://github.com/marianfoo/ui5con24-dev-env/blob/aa4d2051b0b82728a889ebaf431ca2b211af1ac0/mta.yaml#L58

 

The filter will be used to run the command in the related package. The name in the filter is the name of the package in the package.json:

https://github.com/marianfoo/ui5con24-dev-env/blob/aa4d2051b0b82728a889ebaf431ca2b211af1ac0/packages/app/package.json#L2

 

With that, you’re monorepository with multiple packages CAP and UI should be good to go! You can find the full Github repository here: https://github.com/marianfoo/ui5con24-dev-env

entry.title: Understanding and Managing Transport Requests in SAP Central Business Configuration (CBC)
entry.content:
Introduction

In the landscape of SAP Central Business Configuration (CBC), it’s essential to understand how to identify and manage transport requests, especially when changes are made to scoping, organizational units, or business configuration content. This blog will guide you through the steps to effectively handle these transport requests in an SAP S/4HANA Cloud environment, specifically for 3SL systems.

What is a Transport Request?

A transport request is a mechanism in SAP that facilitates the movement of changes made in one system (development) to another (testing or production). In the case of CBC, transport requests are automatically generated when deploying a workspace. These requests document all modifications, allowing for a structured approach to change management.

Understanding Transport Requests in CBC

When you deploy a workspace from the CBC, the system automatically generates a Central Business Configuration (CBC) Type Transport Request. This request captures various changes, such as:

  • Scoping adjustments
  • Modifications to entities
  • Business configuration content alterations

Key Features of CBC Transport Requests:

  • Automatic Creation: Generated during workspace deployment.
  • Automatic Release: Tasks are released automatically post-implementation.

Finding CBC Transport Requests

To locate and manage these transport requests, follow these steps:

  1. Access the Export Customizing Transports App: This app provides a centralized interface for managing transport requests.

  2. Filter by Request Type: Use the "Type" option to specifically filter for requests labeled as "SAP Central Business Configuration." This will help you focus on relevant transport requests.

  3. Check Task Status: Ensure that all tasks associated with the CBC requests have a "released" status. Only requests in this state can be processed further.

Handling CBC Transport Requests

Taking Ownership of a Request

To manage a CBC transport request, you can take ownership by clicking the Assign To Me button. This grants you the necessary permissions to release the request once all associated tasks are complete.

Releasing Customizing Requests

You can release customizing requests using the Export Customizing Transports app. It’s important to note that all tasks within requests of type CBC are automatically released after implementation, ensuring a smooth transition of changes.

Monitoring CBC Staging Status

Transport requests of type Central Business Configuration are linked to a CBC Staging ID. To check the staging status and gather related information, select Display CBC Staging in the transport request overview. This will direct you to the CBC staging area, providing more detailed insights.

If you encounter issues during staging, such as errors or activation failures, it’s essential to report an incident under the component X4-CBC-CW for assistance.

entry.title: Re: Va02 BDC recording to excel file upload program for mass number of sales order
entry.content: Reason: VA02 does the database updates asynchronously (UPDATE 'A'), the lock is released only at the end of the asynchronous updates. You are doing VA02 for sales order 18 twice, once for item 10, once for item 20. You are starting item 20 before item 10 has been processed asynchronously hence the lock on sales order 18 is still active. Doing a Synchronous update instead, means waiting for the end of the whole update processing/lock releasing.

entry.title: Two-tier ERP with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Cloud Edition Overview
entry.content:
This blog aims to define two-tier ERP with SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud Edition and provide details about pre-delivered content that supports different lines of business.

Two-tier ERP overview

Two-tier ERP addresses groups of companies including headquarters and subsidiaries. It comprises a headquarters ERP with high landscape complexity and being highly customized, and a subsidiaries ERP to support quick scale at lower costs with low landscape complexity. Both headquarters and subsidiaries ERP are integrated in a hybrid landscape with suitable implementation and process optimizations.

Two-tier ERP integration content covers connecting SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition to SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition as shown in the image below . Integration content included covering critical and many end-to-end scenarios and can be activated on-demand subjected business requirements. To know more click on " What is two-tier ERP? " page on sap.com


Two-Tier ERP Setup

Two-tier ERP integration between headquarters on SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition and subsidiaries running on SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition might have different needs based on customer preferences

Two-Tier ERP Overview

Let us understand 3 different levels of integration in details

  1. Loosely coupled process – It can address requirements that can be executed in two-tier ERP set up without the real need of system-to-system integration connectivity. For example check the blog on Group Reporting  for more information 
  2. Master Data integration – It is very important to have master data integration specially product master and business partner in both the system . For example check the blog on Master Data Integration  for more information
  3. Tightly coupled process – It can address requirements  that can be executed in Two-tier ERP set up by leveraging SAP delivered communication scenario . For example sales office scenarios

From a functional scope perspective, SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud Edition offers pre-delivered two-tier ERP integrations that cover finance, order-to-cash, and procurement needs of organizations, including those in Professional Services and Manufacturing. These integrations orchestrate end-to-end processes connectivity between connected systems.

To understand how requirements can be categorized into analytics, master data, and process capabilities in a Two-tier ERP with SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud, let's consider the scope of order-to-cash-related requirements

The image above displays the relevant list of scope items available in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud that could address two-tier ERP order to cash related scope .

However, users can explore the given path to understand the full range of SAP-delivered two-tier ERP capabilities  SAP Best Practices for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition and visit

accelerators > Implementation> Two-Tier ERP_End-to-End Business Processes for SAP S/4HANA Cloud 

You can also reach out two-tier ERP community for more clarifications

entry.title: Re: Migrate the ASE database (On-Prem) to the SAP hana Cl...
entry.content:
Hi Namit,

I am not understanding well what you try to achieve, not sure why you need to extract the data model, do you need the DDLs for all the objects ?, do you need them for ASE or for Hana ?. Notice that SAP Advanced SQL migration tool is extracting the data model and keeping it in a sort of internal format to convert it to the target database, the tool is not storing the DDLs to create tables for ASE for instance, it keeps all the columns and other info in text files. For procs and functions it gets the real DDL from the ASE server. Once the extraction is done it is able to convert the data model to Hana and in that case you get the DDLs for tables and other objects.

What do you exactly need ?. ÇASE provides an utility to extract objects and generate the DDLs, take a look at ddlgen, not sure if that may be what you need.

Regards.
Javier.

entry.title: Re: GST Margin scheme on second hand material purchases
entry.content: Hi, Could you please guide our abaper the changes to be made in the routine as suggested by SAP, as our ABAPer could not find the desired logic. The SAP suggestion is as follows: 362 routine is assigned to the tax procedure for condition type BASB which is changing the base value for tax calculation during runtime. Refer attachment for the same. The functionality of 362 is to add up the amount stored in I_KZWI4 and to subtract amount stored in I_KZWI6. 362 is assigned to BASB condition so + or - is happened on the base value. You can refer below KBA 0002474945 - GST IN: Planned and Unplanned delivery cost in GST. Kindly note that SAP hasn't provided any solution on this and it has to be handled at the customer end. I'm giving here some workaround steps, but note that it just a suggestion and no further queries will be addressed on the same. 1. you can write a logic in the routine 362 or 363 to exclude the freight charges (subtotal 4 values - i_kzwi4/i_kzwi6) from the base value (zwrbtr) only for transaction like MIRO, MIR7. 2. Make sure you clear the variable i_kzwi4 or i_kzwi6 after it so it won't get added into next delivery cost. 3. Debug the routine 362 or 363 which is assigned in your tax procedure, you will have a clear idea on tax calculation." Regards Bhanu prakash

entry.title: SAP Stammtisch Melbourne November '24
entry.content:
Every meetup at the Melbourne Stammtisch has been so rewarding. The relaxed atmosphere and the fun and substantial conversation make it so worthwhile. The September Stammtisch was the smallest gathering so far but just as good as always. Callout to @chrisbishop for his consistent support.

This time we will be coinciding with the last day of the TechEd on Tour event (integrated with SAUG-x) that will be occurring a few blocks away at the Melbourne Convention and Exhibition Centre (15 min walk / 5 min Ride Share / 9 min via tram #12 from Clarendon St). We will also start a little earlier at 4:30 for those who might need to get going to the airport afterwards, but come along whenever suits, people normally stick around to around 9:30 or so. This will be a great way to kick back and re-cap the highlights of that event

Make sure you book it in to your calendar (Wednesday 20th November 2024, 4:30pm onwards at The Bank on Collins). You can register your intention to go here but please note that you are welcome to turn up for as short or long as you like. Great food and drinks are available to purchase at the venue. You can ask for the SAP regulars table if you can't find us. Looking forward to see you there.

entry.title: How to Prorate Accruals When Employees Switching Between Regular and Shift Roles?
entry.content:
Managing employee accruals can be complex, especially when employees transition between regular and shift roles within the same year. Understanding how to accurately prorate these accruals is crucial to maintaining accuracy and consistency. Here's a one such example.

Requirement:

When an employee is in a regular role, accruals are determined based on a seniority lookup table. For shift workers, however, the accrual balance is reset to zero.

When an employee transitions between regular and shift roles within a year, the accruals need to be prorated and recalculated accordingly.

For rounding purposes, amounts should be rounded to the nearest 0.5. For example, any amount between 0.01 and 0.50 should be rounded up to 0.5, while amounts between 0.51 and 1.0 should be rounded up to 1.0.

Solution:

  1. We need a custom field on job info to store annual leave entitlement based on whether employee is regular or shift employee. This field will be automatically updated whenever job information is saved.

  1. Accrual Rule– Function to be used in rule-‘Calculate Average Value For Numeric Job Info Field()’ –This rule function calculates the average value of any numeric job information field based on months (that takes care of number of transfers employee might have had during a year). You can prorate or recalculate the leave accrual value depending on the changes to the job information field.

entry.title: Transforming migrations with GenAI - Elevated Operations with SAP Automation Pilot
entry.content:
In the complex world of SAP ECC to S/4HANA migrations, choosing between Greenfield and Brownfield S/4HANA implementation is just the beginning. The real challenge lies in assessing custom code compatibility and planning for a clean core conversion. Sierra Digital enhances significantly this process with their innovative solution, enhanced by the powerful GenAI capabilities of SAP Automation Pilot.

The Power of Sierra Digital’s Solution for Clean Core Assessment

Built on the SAP BTP ABAP environment, Sierra Digital's solution seamlessly accesses SAP Custom Code Analysis findings and generates a comprehensive report. This report scores technical artefacts based on the usage of released/unreleased objects and the availability of fixes—automated or manual—guiding business leaders toward the best migration strategy. However, the process traditionally required significant manual effort, especially from BASIS consultants, to set up SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP)  sub-accounts, services, and instances. Sierra Digital team was looking for a tool to streamline and automate these operations, and I am very excited to share that they managed to do all of that using SAP Automation Pilot capabilities.

What it is SAP Automation Pilot

To setup the right context of the product itself, let’s check what SAP Automaton Pilot is. It is a low-code/no-code automation engine that's part of SAP BTP. SAP Automation Pilot  enables users to create, execute, and manage ops automation workflows. Its main goal is to simplify and automate complex manual processes to minimize the operational effort behind any cloud solution in SAP BTP.

For more detailed information, you can visit the SAP Discovery Center.

Elevating Automation with GenAI

As already announced in this blogpost, Generative AI is available in SAP Automation Pilot. The benefits of introducing GenAI into Automation Pilot are plenty but as of now, the most significant are: reduced time for onboarding and increased usage and variety of use cases covered.

This is why, it is not a surprise, that by leveraging SAP Automation Pilot’s GenAI capabilities, Sierra Digital has significantly reduced the time and effort required for these crucial setup tasks. Automation Pilot’s scripting capabilities allow for the automated creation of sub-accounts, services, and instances, cutting down the need for constant BASIS consultant involvement. Additionally, it ensures consistency across configurations and eliminates the risk of human error.

Perhaps most impressively, Sierra Digital has automated the monitoring of connection disruptions between systems. Through a clever use of Automation Pilot’s provided commands (e.g. HTTP requests) and its integration to SAP Alert Notification service for SAP BTP, any failure in the connection is immediately flagged and communicated. This approach ensures that issues are addressed without delay and without the need for dedicated resources.

The Impact: Efficiency, Accuracy, and Peace of Mind

Thanks to Sierra Digital’s innovative use of SAP Automation Pilot, customers now save valuable time and resources during their SAP S/4HANA migration journeys. Automation of repetitive tasks and real-time monitoring of system connections not only accelerate the process but also provide unparalleled reliability and accuracy. This enhancement to Sierra Digital’s solution is a yet another capability available for businesses looking to transition smoothly and confidently to SAP S/4HANA.

Stay tuned for more insights on how SAP Automation Pilot and GenAI are driving innovation across industries, and discover how your business can benefit from these cutting-edge technologies.

entry.title: Low-Code Machine Learning with Ludwig and SAP AI Core #2-Defect Detection & Sound Anomaly Detection
entry.content:
In my previous blog post, we have discussed the business values, overview and technical details about end-to-end Low-Code Machine Learning with Ludwig and SAP AI Core. In this blog post, I'll continue sharing my experience of replicating another two custom AI use cases from previous work steam with ludwig in low-code approach, comparing with their original implementations in pro-code approach.

Use Case#2: Defect Detection with Image Segmentation

In this use case, we use computer vision(image segmentation) for defect detection in production line of Light Guide Plate. Please refer to our blog post about Build Your Own Model with TensorFlow in SAP AI Core for defect detection and source code for more details. The original implementation is a U-Net model architecture with Tensorflow by my colleague cesarecalabria.

The original training code is available here with 483 lines of python code. Here is just a screenshot of some code snippet. In order to implement this training code, it not only require you to have a good understanding the unet model architecture, but also the skill of ML development with python and tensorflow.

Okay, no worry. Let's implement the same model based on the same dataset with Ludwig in a much easier way.

Dataset and Data Pre-Process

The original dataset is structured as below. The original images of products are stored in Images folder, their corresponding segmented image are store in Masks folder. Sub folders NG(Defected Product) and OK(Passed) are labels of the product image, which is not in used here.

Every product image in Images folder has its own masked pair image in Masks folder. For example

data/Images/NG/00000_NG_Image.bmpdata/Masks/NG/00000_NG_Mask.bmp
Prdocut ImageMask of Defect

The original images in the dataset has a bmp format, which isn't supported in ludwig image feature, hence, we have to transform the original images from bmp format into jpg. Here is the sample code below.

 

import os
from PIL import Image

def convert_bmp_to_jpg(directory):
    # Walk through all files in the directory and its subdirectories
    for root, dirs, files in os.walk(directory):
        for file in files:
            if file.endswith(".bmp"):
                # Create the full file path
                bmp_path = os.path.join(root, file)

                # Open the BMP image
                with Image.open(bmp_path) as img:
                    # Convert the image to RGB mode
                    img = img.convert("RGB")

                    # Create the new file name with .jpg extension
                    jpg_path = os.path.splitext(bmp_path)[0] + ".jpg"

                    # Save the image as a JPG
                    img.save(jpg_path, "JPEG")

                # Optionally, delete the original BMP file after conversion
                os.remove(bmp_path)

                print(f"Converted {bmp_path} to {jpg_path}")

# Replace './data' with the actual path to your directory
convert_bmp_to_jpg("./data")

 

 In addition, we'll also need to create a csv file as dataset.csv based on the image dataset like

 

image_path,mask_path,label
data/Images/NG/00000_NG_Image.jpg,data/Masks/NG/00000_NG_Mask.jpg,NG
data/Images/NG/00001_NG_Image.jpg,data/Masks/NG/00001_NG_Mask.jpg,NG

 

Here is the python script to generate dataset.csv based on the folder structure of dataset

 

import os
import csv

# Define paths
image_dir = "data/Images"
mask_dir = "data/Masks"
output_csv = "dataset.csv"

# Collect all image and mask file paths
image_files = {"NG": [], "OK": []}
mask_files = {"NG": [], "OK": []}

for label in ["NG", "OK"]:
    image_files[label] = sorted([os.path.join(image_dir, label, f) for f in os.listdir(os.path.join(image_dir, label))])
    mask_files[label] = sorted([os.path.join(mask_dir, label, f) for f in os.listdir(os.path.join(mask_dir, label))])

# Create and write to the CSV file
with open(output_csv, mode="w", newline="") as file:
    writer = csv.writer(file)
    writer.writerow(["image", "mask", "label"])

    for label in ["NG", "OK"]:
        for img, mask in zip(image_files[label], mask_files[label]):
            writer.writerow([img, mask, label])

print(f"Dataset CSV file has been created: {output_csv}")

 

 

A declarative config.yaml

Based on the dataset above, all the code we need to train a defect detection model of LPG product with image segmentation is a declarative config.yaml as below. Please refer to this for more details about image feature in ludwig. Literally, we have simplified the training from 480 lines of python code into 40 lines of declarative yaml code. It is way too easy and straight-forward, even application developer and business users with limited machine learning knowledge can write such a declarative config.yam. Having said that, you still need the skill of data pre-processing, and the knowledge of selecting the input and output features based on business objectives and dataset.

 

input_features:
  - name: image_path
    type: image
    preprocessing:
      num_processes: 6
      height: 224
      width: 224
      infer_image_max_height: 1024
      infer_image_max_width: 1024
    encoder: unet

output_features:
  - name: mask_path
    type: image
    preprocessing:
      num_processes: 6
      height: 224
      width: 224
      infer_image_max_height: 1024
      infer_image_max_width: 1024
      infer_image_num_classes: true
      num_channels: 1
      num_classes: 2
    decoder:
      type: unet
      num_fc_layers: 0
    loss:
      type: softmax_cross_entropy
    metric:
      type: iou

combiner:
  type: concat
  num_fc_layers: 0

trainer:
  epochs: 100
  early_stop: -1
  batch_size: 1
  max_batch_size: 1

 

For the model training and serving, you can just use the ludwig command lines, like ludwig experiment/serve etc. which we have gone through in last blog post in detail. So are running the same ludwig workloads within SAP AI Core.

Pro-Code VS Low-Code

Next, let's have a look another use case about audio features.

Use Case#3: Predictive Maintenance based on Machinery Sound Anomaly Detection

In the last use case, we'll detect the sound anomaly of machinery for predictive maintenance of production line. Please refer to our blog post about Sound-based predictive maintenance with SAP AI Core and SAP AI Launchpad and source code for more details. The original implementation is a custom CNN model with Tensorflow by my colleague amagnani.

The original training code is available here with 345 lines of python code. Here is just a screenshot of some code snippet. In order to implement this training code, it not only require you to have a good understanding of CNN, but also the skill of ML development with python and tensorflow.

Okay, no worry. Let's implement the same model based on the same dataset with Ludwig in a much easier way.

Dataset and Data Pre-Process

The original dataset is structured as below. The sub folders anomaly1, anomaly2 and ok are the labels of the audio samples.

We'll also need to create a csv file as dataset.csv based on the image dataset like 

 

audio_path,class
data/ok/clip0568.wav,ok
data/anomaly1/clip0583.wav,anomaly1
data/anomaly2/clip0597.wav,anomaly2

 

Here is the python script to generate dataset.csv based on the folder structure of dataset 

 

import os
import csv

# Define the root directory and the output CSV file
root_dir = "data"
output_csv = "dataset.csv"

# Prepare the data list
data = []

# Traverse the directory structure
for subdir, dirs, files in os.walk(root_dir):
    for file in files:
        if file.endswith(".wav"):  # Filter for .wav files
            # Construct the file path and class label
            file_path = os.path.join(subdir, file)
            class_label = os.path.basename(subdir)
            data.append([file_path, class_label])

# Write to the CSV file
with open(output_csv, "w", newline="") as csvfile:
    writer = csv.writer(csvfile)
    writer.writerow(["audio_path", "class"])  # Write header
    writer.writerows(data)

print(f"CSV file '{output_csv}' generated successfully.")

 

A declarative config.yaml

Based on the dataset above, all the code we need to train a sound anomaly detection with CNN is a declarative config.yaml as below. Please refer to this for more details about image feature in ludwig. Literally, we have simplified the training from 345 lines of python code into 19 lines of declarative yaml code. It is much easier and more straight-forward, even application developers and business users with limited machine learning knowledge can write such a declarative config.yam. Having said that, you still need the skill of data pre-processing, and the knowledge of selecting the input and output features based on business objectives and dataset.

 

input_features:
  - name: audio_path
    type: audio
    preprocessing:
      audio_file_length_limit_in_s: 7.5
      audio_feature:
        type: stft
        window_length_in_s: 0.04
        window_shift_in_s: 0.02
    encoder:
      type: parallel_cnn

output_features:
  - name: class
    type: category

trainer:
  epochs: 100
  early_stop: -1

 

Pro-Code VS Low-Code

Conclusion

In these two use cases, we have witnessed the ease and efficiency of the low-code machine learning with Ludwig and SAP AI Core over the pro-code approach. Ludwig simplifies the machine learning process with a low-code approach. Not only opening the door of machine learning for citizen ML developers like application developer, even business user etc. but also increasing the productivity of the professional machine learning engineers and data scientists to quickly experiment, build, train, and deploy models. When paired with SAP AI Core, these machine learning operations can be streamlined and managed at scale, fostering faster AI-driven innovation across the enterprise with enterprise-grade of security, scalability and compliance.

entry.title: SAP Learning Journey – Administrating SAP Business Technology Platform
entry.content:
Since it was announced at SAP TechEd 2021, SAP offers digital learnings free of charge to everybody interested in SAP products on https://learning.sap.com. In this blog post, I want to present to you the learning journey Administrating SAP Business Technology Platform.

 

Introduction

SAP offers plenty of services to enhance your business in one central place: SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP). This platform-as-a-service (PaaS) provides the central place to improve your handling of data and analytics, integrate data and processes, or develop and extend software by leveraging such SAP BTP services.

But before you can operate a service, you must define the basis like directories, subaccounts, and runtime environments. Users need authorizations and applications need connections to service provides. There are various configuration options to define your service landscape suitable for your business scenarios. All of this should also be observed and monitored concerning performance or costs.

This is exactly what this learning journey is about: Administrating the basis for operating SAP cloud services in SAP BTP. And yes, I chose the term “basis” on purpose for those people out there, who are right now on their journey from on-premise to cloud. Just take a seat in our learning journey.

The units in the current version are the following:

  1. Overview, Responsibilities and Guidance
  2. Architecture
  3. Identity and Access Management
  4. Configuration
  5. Connectivity
  6. Observability and Monitoring
  7. Cost and Usage Management

 

Content

1. Overview, Responsibilities and Guidance

Unit 1 describes shortly the SAP Business Technology Platform and its commercial models. Most importantly, it analyzes responsibilities and guidance resources for SAP BTP.

There is no exercise in this unit. If you want to know more, please check our learning journey Discovering SAP Business Technology Platform acting as a prerequisite.

2. Architecture

Unit 2 covers the SAP BTP account model consisting of global accounts, directories, and subaccounts. After service subscriptions, the runtime environments in SAP BTP are managed. Cloud Foundry and Kyma are enabled and (name-) spaces created to instantiate services in these runtimes. Finaly, the ABAP environment is enabled and the landscape portal is used to manage ABAP system hibernation.

The following is exercised:

  • Create Directories
  • Create a Subaccount in a Directory
  • Create Subaccounts with Default Provider and Region
  • Entitle a Service in a Directory
  • Entitle and Subscribe a Service in a Subaccount
  • Add a Service Plan for Cloud Foundry Environment
  • Enable and Configure a Cloud Foundry Environment
  • Create a Service Instance in a Cloud Foundry
  • Add a Service Plan for Kyma Environment
  • Enable a Kyma Runtime
  • Create a Namespace in a Kyma Runtime
  • Create a Service Instance in Kyma
  • Entitle and Create an ABAP Environment
  • Entitle and Create Web Access for ABAP
  • Create a Subaccount for Operation
  • Entitle, Create, and Authorize the Landscape Portal
  • Check ABAP System Details and Manage System Hibernation

3. Identity and Access Management

Unit 3 explains the identity and access management on SAP BTP on all levels and distinguishes business from platform users. How to manage users and authorizations on SAP BTP  with roles and role collections connected to identity providers is followed by a summary of the security concept for cloud native applications. This leads to the management of users and authorizations in SAP BTP environments.

The following is exercised:

  • Create Administrator User in a Subaccount
  • Inspect and Assign Predefined Role Collections for Applications
  • Assign Developer Authorizations in Cloud Foundry
  • Manage Cluster Roles in Kyma
  • Manage Business Roles in the ABAP Environment

4. Configuration

Unit 4 provides various ways to configure the SAP BTP starting with boosters, which are guided interactive steps to configure services. Operating the command line interface (CLI) tools btp CLI for SAP BTP and cf CLI for Cloud Foundry are like the kubectl for Kyma the most direct way to interact with SAP BTP on a technical level. To manage SAP BTP with Terraform incorporates btp CLI to automate infrastructure provisioning and management.

The following is exercised:

  • Start a Booster in SAP BTP
  • Operate SAP BTP Command Line Interface (btp CLI)
  • Setting and Configuring Kubectl for Kyma
  • Create a Terraform Configuration for SAP BTP

5. Connectivity

Unit 5 starts with analyzing connectivity in SAP BTP and jumps to using destinations on subaccount, service, and environment level. To connect the SAP BTP to on-premise systems, operating the cloud connector is mandatory for any SAP BTP service.

The following is exercised:

  • Create a Destination
  • How to Install Cloud Connector on Linux
  • Replace the UI Certificate in Cloud Connector
  • How to Configure First SAP BTP Subaccount in Cloud Connector
  • Configure Access Control (HTTP)
  • How to Use a Cloud to On-Premise Scenario

6. Observability and Monitoring

Unit 6 imparts the observability strategy of SAP BTP with SAP Cloud Application Lifecycle Management (ALM) in its core. It offers central observability tools like business process, integration, user, or health monitoring. Including the local observability tools SAP Alert Notification Service and SAP Automation Pilot, SAP BTP offers a comprehensive monitoring approach.

There is no exercise in this unit. If you want to know more, please check our learning journey Operating with SAP Cloud ALM, especially unit 2 (Using Monitoring and Analysis).

7. Cost and Usage Management

Unit 7 closes the learning journey with cost and usage management, a topic often requested by customers.

There is no exercise in this unit but many slides and videos with hints around handling the costs of SAP BTP efficiently.

 

Summary

If you have read so far, it seems that you are really thinking about consuming the learning journey. Don’t hesitate and jump right in:

https://learning.sap.com/learning-journeys/administrating-sap-business-technology-platform

If you want to put your hands on some live system, the SAP BTP trial is just on click away:

https://www.sap.com/products/technology-platform/pricing.html

If you are more interested in a customer course for SAP BTP administration, please visit https://training.sap.com. The ADMCLD (Introduction to SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) Administration) is a 3-day-course offered as physical and virtual event:

https://training.sap.com/course/admcld

For any other questions around the offerings of SAP Learning Services, please jump to our community page and get in contact with us:

https://pages.community.sap.com/topics/training-certification

Happy learning
Stefan

entry.title: Re: How to maintain Priority in Purchase requisition and...
entry.content:
Hi,

Refer to the KBA https://me.sap.com/notes/0003210447

You would like to set different work item priorities based on your own custom logic for Procurement documents.
For this purpose you can use the BADI MMPUR_WFL_CONTEXT_ENHANCE.

For Purchase Requisitions you are able to set the work item priority but for Purchase Orders the work item priorities cannot be set. 

The BADI MMPUR_WFL_CONTEXT_ENHANCE is only fully enabled for Purchase Requisitions.
Setting a work item priority for Purchase Orders via the BADI is not yet enabled.  

Best regards,

SAP Support

entry.title: What’s New in SAP Central Business Configuration 2409
entry.content:
With the latest release for SAP Central Business Configuration, exciting new features are available. This blog post will provide you with a comprehensive overview of these features.

Improved Configuration Activities Filtering Based on Country Relevance

In the Configure Your Business Processes app, a new filter allows you to screen configuration activities based on their significance for specific countries or regions in scope. For example, it’s now possible to filter configuration activities that are relevant to one or multiple countries/regions in scope or globally. This functionality allows you to quickly identify the right configuration activities, thereby saving you valuable time.

Screenshot of the Country/Region filter in the Configure Your Business Processes app

Enhanced User Experience Within Change Logs

In the Change Logs app, we’ve enhanced the search functionality: The Changed By column now includes a filter where you can enter a username. This enhancement helps you quickly pinpoint changes made by a particular user, thereby saving you valuable time. Note that case sensitivity is not an issue as the search is not case-sensitive.

Screenshot of the Changed By column filter in the Change Logs app

For a quick summary, check out this video about What's New in 2409. Don’t forget to also use the What’s New Viewer on the SAP Help Portal, which provides further details and links about all the recent changes.

That wraps up the new features this time. Make sure to follow the SAP Central Business Configuration tag or follow this dedicated blog collection to stay up to date with the latest blog posts.

Do you have an idea on how to further enhance SAP Central Business Configuration? Go to the SAP Customer Influence portal to submit an improvement request or vote for the ideas of others. This helps us understand your requirements even better and prioritize our work accordingly. We’re looking forward to your improvement requests.

entry.title: Re: Digital Skills Initiative Applicant Guide (For self-study candidates)
entry.content:
Hello @David_Chaviano,

My name is Sameer, and I would like to express my heartfelt gratitude to SAP for this incredible initiative.

I am eager to contribute and be part of Digital Skills Initiative, with a particular interest in SAP Fiori. Unfortunately, I had to take a temporary break from my career to care for my parents, but I am now ready to restart my journey as an SAP developer.

With prior experience in the manufacturing industry, I have developed a strong foundation in Java, JavaScript, and programming concepts such as OOPS and RESTful APIs. I am excited to apply these skills to SAP Fiori and make a meaningful impact.

As someone who identifies as a first-generation college graduate and lacks the financial means for certification attempts, I would greatly appreciate any advice or opportunities that could help me move forward in this field.

Best regards,
Sameer

entry.title: Sales Office scenarios in Two-Tier ERP
entry.content:
Reflecting on the recently concluded Two-Tier ERP Confluence event, where we focused on integration and various sales office scenarios, this blog will bring a business perspective to the following key sales office scenarios and their significance:

  • Sale from Local Sales Office (Goods and Basic Services)
  • Sale from Central Stock, Direct Delivery to Customer from HQ
  • Returns Handling at Sales Office, with Credit and Debit Memo
  • Sale from HQ, Direct Procurement from Local Sales Office
  • Services from Sales Office for HQ
  • Installation, Warranty, Repair from Sales Office
  • Predictive Maintenance with AIN from Sales Office

Now, let’s take a deeper look at the different business scenarios, particularly focusing on sales from local offices.

A local sales office scenario is a prime example of loosely coupled scenario, which has been highlighted in the Two-Tier ERP Overview. In contrast, other sales office cases often involve dependent subsidiaries, which can be referred as tightly coupled scenario. You can find the relevant info here in the Two-Tier ERP Overview. Regardless of the structural differences between loosely coupled and tightly coupled, most of these scenarios can be grouped into two main categories:

  1. Inventory Sales and Returns Management
  2. Sale of services

Among the scenarios, listed above, the sale from the local sales office is well-known, but understanding why this scenario is crucial from a business perspective requires a deeper dive. This scenario enables regional sales offices to operate autonomously, driving localized decision-making, faster customer response times, and greater flexibility in meeting market demands. It is particularly beneficial when dealing with different compliance requirements, currency, or language variations across regions.

The Drop-Ship Scenario: An End-to-End Perspective

Let's now turn our attention to one of the most common sales processes from End-to-End perspective is “sale from central stock with direct delivery to the customer from headquarters”, commonly known as Drop-Ship scenario. This process offers significant advantages in maintaining lean inventories at sales offices while ensuring customer satisfaction with direct deliveries.

The following is an End-to-End scenario that offers detailed process information, outlining where each step is executed within a business context.

Future Exploration

In the upcoming blogs, we will take a deeper dive into each of the process steps outlined in the End-to-End scenario, exploring different variants and how they can benefit the business.

We will also take a deeper look in to Returns Management, in future blogs.

Sale of Services in a Two-Tier ERP Deployment

As outlined earlier, in Fig 1, the sale of services is another key scenario. For the sale of services, as outlined above, local subsidiaries manage both customer interaction and service delivery, allowing for greater agility and a better understanding of local needs. The Two-Tier ERP deployment model enables seamless integration between the local and HQ systems, ensuring smooth data flow across the organization.

For more information on warranty management, you can refer the blog here: https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blogs-by-sap/warranty-claim-process-in-headquarter-subsidiary-model-for-two-tier-erp/ba-p/13743032

You can also reach out two-tier ERP community for more clarifications
